All nine isolates that over-expressed cmeB contained a mutation in cmeR, substituting glycine 86 with alanine. All cmeB over-expressing isolates also accumulated low concentrations of ciprofloxacin, which were restored to wild-type levels in the presence of CCCP. It requires a complete and fully functional efflux pump cmeABC.

During in vitro selection of erythromycin-resistant mutants, this insertion increased the MIC for erythtomycin from 1 mg/L to 32-512 mg/L (18779354). Also validated by site-directed mutagenesis, where this insertion increases the MIC for erythromycin from 4 mg/L to 256 mg/L (16940070).

cmeR is a repressor of the efflux pump cmeABC.
